title: Egyptian Lentil Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Egyptian dish featuring lentils, cilantro, and a glassy jelly texture, perfect for a lazy Sunday, this recipe is shojin-friendly and made in a paella pan.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a paella pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the red lentils, vegetable broth, tomato paste, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes.
Stir in the chopped cilantro and lemon juice.
Continue to simmer for an additional 10 minutes, or until the lentils are tender and the mixture has a thick, glassy texture.
Remove the paella pan from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Serve the Egyptian Lentil Tagine hot, garnished with additional chopped cilantro if desired.
